memberdef.argsstring: (float *o_out, const float *q_all, const float *kv_cache_k, const float *kv_cache_v, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int head_dim, int seq_len, int kv_tile_size)
memberdef.name: fused_flash_attention_all_heads

briefdescription.para: Fused Flash Attention for all heads (parallel dispatch)

detaileddescription.para: Dispatches per-head attention to parallel cores. Each head's working set fits in L1.

parameterdescription.para: Q tensor for all heads [num_heads * head_dim]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *o_out, const float *q, const float *kv_cache_k, const float *kv_cache_v, int kv_head_idx, int seq_len, int head_dim, int kv_tile_size)
memberdef.name: fused_flash_attention_head

briefdescription.para: Fused Flash Attention for single head.

detaileddescription.para: Online softmax with streaming KV tiles. O, m, l stay in registers throughout.

parameterdescription.para: Which KV head this Q head uses

briefdescription.para: Compute optimal KV tile size for flash attention.

detaileddescription.para: Uses 80% of L1 to leave room for prefetch and OS.

parameterdescription.para: L1 cache size in bytes (typically 49152 for 48KB)
Element size (2 for FP16, 4 for FP32)

simplesect.para: Optimal KV tile size (multiple of 64 for cache line alignment)

memberdef.argsstring: (int l1_size, int head_dim, int kv_tile_size, int bytes_per_elem)
memberdef.name: fused_kernels_validate_constraints

simplesect.para: 0 if valid, -1 if working set exceeds cache

memberdef.argsstring: (float *output, const float *o_all, const float *W_o, const float *b_o, const float *residual, int hidden, int num_heads, int head_dim)
memberdef.name: fused_output_projection_residual

briefdescription.para: Fused output projection with residual add.

detaileddescription.para: Computes O @ W_o + residual, all in registers/L1. Final store includes residual add.

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *gamma, const float *beta, float *output, int hidden, float eps)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm

briefdescription.para: Fused RMSNorm - writes to pre-allocated buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Takes input, applies RMSNorm, writes to output buffer. Buffer should be in L1/L2 for best performance.

parameterdescription.para: Beta parameter [hidden] or NULL (RMSNorm doesn't use beta typically)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *gamma, const float *W_qkv, const float *b_qkv, float *q_out, float *k_out, float *v_out, int hidden, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int head_dim, float eps)
memberdef.name: fused_rmsnorm_qkv

briefdescription.para: Fused RMSNorm with fused QKV projection.

detaileddescription.para: Computes RMSNorm and immediately projects to Q, K, V. No intermediate buffer - Q/K/V written directly to output buffers.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, const float *rope_cos, const float *rope_sin, int pos, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int head_dim, int max_seq)
memberdef.name: fused_rope_inplace

briefdescription.para: Fused RoPE application (in-place on pre-allocated buffers)

detaileddescription.para: Applies RoPE rotation to Q and K in their buffers. Buffers stay in cache - no extra memory allocation.

parameterdescription.para: Q tensor [num_heads * head_dim] - modified in place
K tensor [num_kv_heads * head_dim] - modified in place

memberdef.argsstring: (float *output, const float *input, const float *residual, const float *W_qkv, const float *b_qkv, const float *W_o, const float *b_o, float *kv_cache_k, float *kv_cache_v, const float *rope_cos, const float *rope_sin, int pos, int seq_len, int hidden, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int head_dim, int max_seq, float eps)
memberdef.name: mega_fused_attention

detaileddescription.para: RMSNorm → QKV → RoPE → Flash Attention → OutProj + Residual
All intermediates in L1/L2/registers. Single DRAM round-trip: 4KB in + 4KB out.

parameterdescription.para: KV cache K [seq, hidden] - updated in place
KV cache V [seq, hidden] - updated in place

briefdescription.para: Fused Kernel API for Cache-Aware Attention Fusion.

detaileddescription.para:
Key design principles:
Kernels take output buffer as parameter (no internal malloc)
Buffers are designed to fit in L1/L2 cache
Kernels can chain: rmsnorm → QKV → RoPE → Flash → OutProj
Per-head parallelization with L1 cache constraints

Cache hierarchy (typical Xeon):
Registers: 32 ZMM × 64B = 2KB (AVX-512)
L1: 48KB per core
L2: 1-2MB per core
L3: 1.5MB × cores (shared)
Per-head working set must fit in L1: Q_h (128B) + K_tile (8KB) + V_tile (8KB) + O_h (256B) = ~16.8KB
